join_order.probe: num(filtered: uom='USD', value IS NOT NULL)
join_order.notes: Use sub_sic_postings to seed a tiny direct lookup so only qualifying filings reach any later work.

join_order.build: pre(filtered stmt='EQ' and adsh in qualifying sub set; (adsh,tag,version) -> eq_count, plus deduplicated reachable (tag,version) pairs)
join_order.notes: Keep the compact triple-count map from the filtered EQ rows and simultaneously collect the much smaller reachable tag/version domain.

join_order.build: tag(filtered to reachable (tag,version); (tag,version) -> tlabel where abstract=0)
join_order.notes: Probe the persisted tag_tag_version_hash once per reachable pair during build_joins, then replace per-row probes of the 76MB persisted tag structure with a compact cache-resident pair->tlabel map.

data_structures.dim_filter: direct_array(max_adsh_code+1, value=sub_rowid_or_sentinel) populated from sub_sic_postings for SIC 4000..4999
data_structures.fact_join: compact_hash_map(pre_sized by first pass over pre_stmt_postings restricted to qualifying adsh values, key=(adsh_u32,tag_u32,version_u32), value=eq_count_u32) plus compact_hash_map(pre_sized by deduplicated reachable pairs, key=(tag_u32,version_u32), value=tlabel_u32); use tag_tag_version_hash only during build_joins to populate the filtered pair map
data_structures.aggregation: compact_hash_map(thread_local, key=(sic_i32,tlabel_u32,stmt_u16,cik_i32), value={sum_value:f64,count_rows:u64}); finalize by rolling up to (sic_i32,tlabel_u32,stmt_u16) and deriving COUNT(DISTINCT cik) from one company-grain entry per key

indexes.purpose: enumerate qualifying sub rows in SIC range and seed the filtered adsh lookup
enumerate only EQ pre rows for the filtered triple-count build and reachable tag/version collection
scan only USD num row ids
resolve each reachable (tag,version) pair once during build_joins and materialize a compact pair->tlabel map for the hot scan

optimization_notes: Root cause: after iteration 1, the hot path still does cache-unfriendly random probes into the 4194304-bucket tag hash (about 64MB of buckets plus offsets/rowids, roughly 76MB total) and also maintains DISTINCT bookkeeping in a second hash structure even though the sub and pre filters shrink the reachable domain to at most 64656 filtered triples. Strategy: Category A/C. Keep the selective sub->pre pruning, but during build_joins also deduplicate the reachable (tag,version) pairs and probe the persisted tag index once per pair to build a compact cache-resident pair->tlabel map; then scan USD num rows using only the filtered sub/pre/tag lookups and aggregate directly at (sic,tlabel,stmt,cik) grain so COUNT(DISTINCT cik) is fused into aggregation and rolled up once at finalize instead of maintaining and merging a separate distinct set.
